chunk_summary.idea_trajectory_justification: The first seven minutes are entirely procedural (introductions, assigning roles), while the final three minutes shift into divergent exploration of open science and data sharing.
chunk_summary.collective_engagement_justification: Participants maintain visual attention on the active speaker and provide occasional head nods, particularly during the discussion on open science.
chunk_summary.cross_disciplinary_bridging_description: Compared data sharing practices in astronomy to those in protein dynamics.
chunk_summary.problem_specificity_justification: The group narrowed the topic to open science and data sharing, but has not articulated a specific research question.
chunk_summary.decision_crystallization_justification: The group is just beginning to explore general topics and has not identified a shared project direction.
chunk_summary.funding_reference_description: Participants noted that sharing negative data is not typically rewarded by current funding and promotion structures.
chunk_summary.notable_observation: The group spent an unusually long time (over 7 minutes) on procedural tasks and introductions before addressing the substantive prompt.

ideas_currently_on_table: Open science and sharing negative data to fuel AI
chunk_summaries: The group spent the first seven minutes on procedural tasks, including assigning scribe and reporter roles, locating the shared notes document, and conducting a round of introductions for all 15 participants. Erika then outlined the agenda to discuss what works and doesn't work in current scientific frameworks, prompting Stephen to introduce the idea of open science and the need to share negative data to fuel future AI breakthroughs. The tone was polite and structured, with the group ending the chunk in the early stages of divergent exploration regarding systemic issues in data sharing and funding incentives.
